project.name: DailyDex
project.description: Flask dashboard for news scoring, curation, and intelligence

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Containerization recipe that builds the Python environment for the DailyDex application, installs system dependencies (including Gemini CLI), installs Python requirements, and exposes port 8888 with Gunicorn serving src.dashboard_new:app.

nodes.name: python
nodes.summary: The main python:3.11-slim execution stage that sets up the environment variables, runs pip installs, and starts the gunicorn dashboard server.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Tracks chronological changes, release history, and feature additions for the DailyDex project, spanning versions v0.6.0 through v0.15.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Outlines guidelines, local development workflows, Docker setup, coding conventions, and required checks for contributing to the DailyDex repository.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary documentation for DailyDex (Creator Cockpit), describing its core capabilities, showcases, quick start setup (Docker/local), configuration settings, and development guides.

nodes.name: SECURITY.md
nodes.summary: Defines the security policy for DailyDex, outlining supported versions, how to report vulnerabilities, and sensitive project areas.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Main configuration file containing variant definitions, feeds, and settings for Telegram, arXiv, YouTube, GitHub, Hugging Face, and various blogs.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard JSON file used to map structural settings and configuration objects.

nodes.name: deploy.sh
nodes.summary: Deployment script used to automate environment variables synchronization with Vercel and handle deployment tasks.
nodes.languageNotes: Zsh/Bash shell script automating environmental config synchronizations and deployments.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Lists Python package dependencies required for the DailyDex application, including database clients and scraper libraries.

nodes.name: runtime.txt
nodes.summary: Specifies the target Python runtime version for hosting/deployment environments.

nodes.name: supabase_schema.sql
nodes.summary: Database schema definition for Supabase, establishing tables, relational integrity, indexes, and performance constraints across all core features.
nodes.languageNotes: Supabase SQL schema defining schema structures, tracking indexes, and relational integrity constraints.

nodes.name: vercel.json
nodes.summary: Vercel hosting configuration containing routing rules, cron job definitions, rewrites, and environment variable settings for serverless functions.

nodes.name: saved_items
nodes.summary: Core table storing saved content items, curation metrics (signal/creator scores), outline structures, priority, and production/publication statuses.

nodes.name: trend_history
nodes.summary: Tracks occurrence counts and first/last seen timestamps of trend sources over time.

nodes.name: intelligence_clusters
nodes.summary: Stores grouped content clusters represented as JSON, tracking when they were created.

nodes.name: ignored_items
nodes.summary: Maintains record of ignored content URLs and titles along with the reason for rejection to prevent reprocessing.

nodes.name: tracked_topics
nodes.summary: Tracks specific topics of interest, reasons for tracking, and notification preferences.

nodes.name: source_health
nodes.summary: Monitors the synchronization and operational health of ingestion sources, detailing failures, success rates, and caching status.

nodes.name: seen_items
nodes.summary: Keeps a history of all URLs and titles seen by scrapers to optimize deduplication across runs.

nodes.name: telegram_subscribers
nodes.summary: Stores active subscribers of the Telegram bot notification system.

nodes.name: item_votes
nodes.summary: Tracks voter feedback on ingested items via the Telegram integration.

nodes.name: creator_assets
nodes.summary: Stores generated creative payloads, model metadata, error logs, and origin URLs indexed by content hash.

nodes.name: cluster_snapshots
nodes.summary: Aggregates cluster intelligence snapshots over specific hourly buckets, summing signal values and referencing source configurations.

nodes.name: agent_runs
nodes.summary: Tracks status, progress, stage, ETA, and execution summaries of background curation and generation agents.

nodes.name: agent_logs
nodes.summary: Stores detailed execution logs linked to specific agent runs.

nodes.name: schedule
nodes.summary: Schedules execution tasks for content items, tracking publication day, time, type, and deployment status.

nodes.name: thumbnail_variants
nodes.summary: Maintains variations of generated thumbnails, including color properties, picked flags, and predicted click-through rates (CTR).

nodes.name: studio_content
nodes.summary: Stores drafted content models, research summaries, source URLs, and generation status for the studio workspace.

nodes.name: publication_analytics
nodes.summary: Tracks views, impressions, CTR, engagement rate, and publication status for distributed content across social platforms.

nodes.name: repurposed_clips
nodes.summary: Stores repurposed short-form clips created from parent content, including video intervals and virality prediction scores.

nodes.name: ab_tests
nodes.summary: Manages title and thumbnail A/B tests, recording impressions, views, and CTR metrics for different variants.

nodes.name: creator_profile.json
nodes.summary: Defines the content creator's target niche, audience persona, banned phrases, formatting requirements, and publishing schedule, along with configurations for agentic automation and LLM copilots.

nodes.name: runtime.json
nodes.summary: Configuration file managing fetch and email synchronization intervals, cache policies, LLM endpoints, active user interface features, Raspberry Pi resource optimization, and local data storage paths.

nodes.name: sources.json
nodes.summary: Specifies active external information feeds including Github trending filters, HuggingFace download-based limits, YouTube channel listings, RSS feeds, and arXiv categories.

nodes.name: topics.json
nodes.summary: Declares focus areas, blocked keywords, scoring weights, and threshold categories used to prioritize, evaluate, and filter retrieved tech news articles.

nodes.name: release_validation_v0.7.md
nodes.summary: Release validation checklist for the DailyDex v0.7.0-rc1 release candidate, covering UI navigation, light/dark theme toggle, data actions, daily digest, and responsive layout tests.

nodes.name: release_validation_v0.9.md
nodes.summary: Release validation checklist for the DailyDex v0.9 release, detailing validation tests for Creator Mode, Daily Digest, saved boards, bulk actions, and responsive layout.

nodes.name: ui_review_checklist.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive UI review checklist for the DailyDex v0.6 Modern Dashboard, outlining evaluation criteria for navigation layout, card readability, theme system, accessibility, and visual design consistency.

nodes.name: ai-coding-agents-2026.md
nodes.summary: YouTube video script outlining the landscape of AI coding agents in 2026, focusing on model routing, MCP architecture, and prompt caching.

nodes.name: youtube_ai_coding_agents_war.md
nodes.summary: YouTube video script discussing the mid-2026 agent 'war phase,' comparing new releases from Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI, and MiniMax.

nodes.name: release_check.sh
nodes.summary: Release check shell script validating Python/JavaScript syntax, executing Docker build, and running unit tests via pytest.
nodes.languageNotes: Bash shell script using py_compile, node --check, docker, and pytest for validation.

tour.title: Welcome to DailyDex
tour.description: Start here to understand the architecture and core purpose of DailyDex as a Flask-based news intelligence and curation dashboard. We explore the entry points of the application, including the main README and the serverless API router on Vercel.

tour.title: Supabase Relational Database Schema
tour.description: Examine the Postgres database schema that powers DailyDex. Learn how tables like saved_items, creator_assets, agent_runs, and ab_tests maintain state across scrapers, curation tasks, and publication actions.
tour.languageLesson: PostgreSQL data definitions, indexing strategies for performance, and using relational keys to manage background process states.

tour.title: Configuring Sources, Topics, and Personas
tour.description: Understand how the curation engine evaluates incoming feeds. We explore config.json and the JSON files under the config/ directory which define active content sources, topic keywords, evaluation weights, and the target audience profile for the creator.

tour.title: News Ingestion Engine
tour.description: Dive into how DailyDex fetches tech news and content across GitHub trending repositories, YouTube channels, arXiv papers, RSS feeds, and Telegram chats, preparing them for deduplication and scoring.

tour.title: Scoring and Filtering Algorithms
tour.description: Explore the heuristics and formulas used to compute quality scores for raw ingested articles. The scoring engine filters out spam and boosts high-signal tech articles using configured topic weights and metrics.

tour.title: Topic Clustering and Trend Detection
tour.description: Learn how DailyDex groups related articles into high-level intelligence clusters. This module helps identify emerging tech trends and aggregates multiple sources covering the same event to reduce noise.

tour.title: Agentic Curation and Research Packs
tour.description: See how background agents automatically enrich saved items and build comprehensive research packs. The researcher queries LLMs to synthesize technical summaries, 'why it matters' annotations, and suggested action items.

tour.title: Creator Studio Draft Generation
tour.description: Examine how the application transforms raw research packs into ready-to-use creative assets. The studio coordinates LLM workflows to draft scripts, outlines, and summaries based on the creator's profile.

tour.title: LLM System Skill Templates
tour.description: Inspect the system-level markdown files that teach AI agents how to write high-quality blog posts, podcast scripts, and video shorts. These templates enforce style guidelines, tone parameters, and length restrictions.

tour.title: The Flask Dashboard Backend
tour.description: Analyze the core server code in dashboard_new.py. This file registers settings managers, exposes REST APIs for save/delete actions, and serves as the server backend for the frontend React single-page app.

tour.title: Creator Cockpit Frontend
tour.description: Walk through the UI layer of the application. The React application displays interactive feeds, research packs, active content generation pipelines, scheduling calendars, and thumbnail options.
tour.languageLesson: React dynamic views, component state management using hooks, and rendering of status dashboards based on active API schemas.

tour.title: Remotion Video Engine
tour.description: Discover how DailyDex generates video shorts programmatically using React. The Remotion subproject takes structured video properties (images, audio, text clips) and renders finished video files for publication.
tour.languageLesson: Programmatic video rendering with Remotion, composing video tracks dynamically in TypeScript, and audio-visual synchronization.

tour.title: Dockerization and Serverless Deployment
tour.description: Look at the deployment setups. DailyDex can run containerized via Docker on local hardware or be deployed serverlessly to Vercel, using cron endpoints to schedule periodic content refreshes and enrichments.
